description: A dashboard to visualize Cats Effect runtime metrics.

panels.description: The percent of time spent on Garbage Collection over all CPUs assigned to the JVM process.
panels.title: GC Pressure

options.content:
The [starvation checker](https://typelevel.org/cats-effect/docs/core/starvation-and-tuning) is a runtime assertion that any latency higher than the threshold (by default, 100 milliseconds) is bad and deserves a warning. The common causes:
- Blocking tasks
- Compute-bound work
- Not enough CPUs
- Too many threads
- Burst credit contention
- Process contention

panels.description: The current clock drift
panels.title: Clock drift [current]

panels.description: The current number of time CPU starvation has occurred
panels.title: Starvations [$range]

panels.description: The current (last) and max (since the launch) observed clock drift in milliseconds
panels.title: Clock drift

options.content:
This dashboard evaluates the utilization of compute threads in the [Cats Effect](https://github.com/typelevel/cats-effect) runtime:
- Thread pool utilization - monitor the utilization of the worker threads
- Blocking and searching threads - identify bottlenecks by tracking blocked and searching threads
- Fiber management - understand fiber enqueue trends

panels.description: The number of worker thread instances backing the work-stealing thread pool (WSTP)
panels.title: Worker threads

panels.description: The number of active worker thread instances currently executing fibers on the compute thread pool
panels.title: Active threads

panels.description: The number of worker thread instances that can run blocking actions on the compute thread pool
panels.title: Blocking threads

panels.description: The number of active worker thread instances currently executing fibers on the compute thread pool
panels.title: Active

panels.description: The number of worker thread instances that can run blocking actions on the compute thread pool
panels.title: Blocking

panels.description: The number of worker thread instances currently searching for fibers to steal from other worker threads
panels.title: Searching

panels.description: The total number of fibers enqueued on all local queues
panels.title: Enqueued fibers [$range]

panels.description: The number of fibers which are currently asynchronously suspended
panels.title: Suspended fibers [$range]

panels.description: The total number of events that happend to the worker thread
panels.title: Events per state [$range]

panels.description: The total amount of time in nanoseconds that this worker thread has been parked
panels.title: Idle (parked) duration [$range]

panels.description: The number of currently enqueued fibers
panels.title: Enqueued fibers

panels.description: The total number of fibers enqueued during the lifetime of the local queue
panels.title: Total fibers [$range]

panels.description: The total number of stolen fibers by other worker threads
panels.title: Stolen fibers [$range]

panels.description: The total number of successful steal attempts by other worker threads
panels.title: Successful steal attempts [$range]

panels.description: The total number of fibers spilled over to the external queue
panels.title: Spilled over fibers [$range]

panels.description: The time in nanoseconds till the next due to fire
panels.title: Next due

panels.description: The current number of the outstanding timers that remain to be executed
panels.title: Outstanding timers

panels.description: The total number of timers per state
panels.title: Timers [$range]

panels.description: The total number of times the heap packed itself to remove canceled timers
panels.title: Packed [$range]

panels.description: The current number of outstanding operations per category
panels.title: Outstanding operations

panels.description: The total number of operations per category and outcome
panels.title: Total operations [$range]
